pattern: Recoding every value below k as -1, above k as +1, and k itself as 0 turns 'median equals k' into a balance condition on a running sum around k's fixed position, so counting matching balances on each side with a hash map finds every valid subarray in one pass.
hints: Since nums holds distinct integers from 1 to n, first find the single index where the value k sits -- every valid subarray must include that index.

Recode the array: any value less than k becomes -1, any value greater than k becomes +1, and k itself becomes 0. A subarray containing k's index has median k exactly when its running balance (count of +1s minus count of -1s) is 0 or 1, depending on whether the subarray length is odd or even.

Walk outward from k's index in both directions, tracking the running balance from k to the right and separately from k to the left; count, with a hash map, how many left-side balances pair with each right-side balance to make the whole subarray's balance land on 0 or 1.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, only the singleton subarray qualifies
k sits at the very first index
k sits at the very last index
already sorted ascending run, tests even-length median convention (left-of-center)
already sorted descending run, same even-length convention from the other direction
k in the middle with a symmetric spread of smaller and larger values around it

stress.description: random permutations of 1..n with k drawn from the same range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    perm = list(range(1, n + 1))
    rng.shuffle(perm)
    k = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [perm, k]
